checks.a_analysis: The output JSON faithfully represents the content of the source presentation. All headings, bullet points, and paragraphs match the text provided in the source images and OCR. There is no evidence of fabricated content. The title 'Q10: Differential Privacy Impacts' is a reasonable normalization for a section heading based on the slide's content.

I cross-referenced all email addresses on the final slide (Page 16) of the source document with Section 16 of the output JSON. The email addresses 'DABrannan@cabarruscounty.us', 'anna.verrill@nc.gov', and 'tim.johnson@nc.gov' are correctly captured and match the source perfectly.

The source document contains 16 slides/pages. All 16 pages are accounted for in the 16 sections of the JSON output. Every section's content (survey questions, listservs, summary points, etc.) is present.
The source document does not contain any data tables; it uses bulleted lists and charts. The lists in the output are complete and correspond to the source.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: The text within each section is complete. Long paragraphs, such as the one on Page 13 regarding Differential Privacy and the detailed list on Page 11, have been captured in full without material omission.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The output maintains high fidelity to the source text. Formatting choices like using superscript for 'March 11th' in S2 or correctly capturing the italicized text on the title page are noted. There are no wording changes that alter the meaning of the government document.

checks.a_analysis: The document conversion was thoroughly checked for repeated text blocks. Each section and slide content (headings, paragraphs, lists) from the 16-slide presentation appears only once in the JSON output, corresponding exactly with its single occurrence in the source PDF. No duplicated paragraphs or repeated list items were identified.

The JSON uses a logical heading hierarchy. Section titles correspond to the titles of the presentation slides. Within the first section, which represents the cover slide, the main text components ('Local Government Committee' and 'Census Data Survey Results') are appropriately tagged as H2. The hierarchy is flat and consistent, with no skipping of heading levels (e.g., no H1 to H3 jumps).

The source document contains charts (bar and pie charts) rather than traditional data tables. These are correctly captured as images with descriptive alt text rather than attempting to force them into a table structure. There are no actual tables in the source to be corrupted.
The reading order in the JSON follows the linear sequence of the slides in the PDF (pages 1 through 16). The flow of information is logical and mirrors the intended presentation order.

The presentation relies heavily on bulleted lists. These have been accurately converted. Specifically, the complex multi-level nested list on Page 2 ('Survey Background') is correctly represented with nested list structures in the JSON. The two-column list on Page 11 ('Q8') is correctly flattened into a single semantic list, which is the preferred conversion for accessibility.
The email contact information on Page 16 has been correctly identified and converted into active 'mailto:' links in the JSON. No broken or fabricated links were found.

checks.a_analysis: The converted HTML document faithfully follows the visual organization of the source PDF. The original presentation slides are rendered as sequential sections in a single-column layout, which is an appropriate responsive conversion for a slide deck. Headers and lists are placed correctly relative to each other, maintaining the visual flow and intent of the original document.
The source document does not contain any formal data tables. It consists of text, bulleted lists, and charts. Therefore, this check is not applicable.

Several meaningful data visualizations (charts) present in the source PDF are missing from the HTML output. Specifically, the bar charts on pages 4, 5, 6, and 8, and the pie chart on page 10 are absent. While text descriptions summarizing the charts have been provided in the HTML, the original graphical images that constitute the primary content of those sections are missing, representing a major loss in visual fidelity.
checks.b_evidence_source: The source PDF contains meaningful data charts on pages 4 (Q1 bar chart), 5 (Q2 bar chart), 6 (Q3 bar chart), 8 (Q5 bar chart), and 10 (Q7 pie chart).
checks.b_evidence_output: The HTML output shows text descriptions (e.g., "Bar chart showing responses to...") but lacks the actual images or graphical renderings of these charts.
checks.b_evidence_location: Sections Q1, Q2, Q3, Q5, and Q7

checks.a_analysis: There is no evidence of visual content duplication in the body of the converted document. The document title appearing at the top and again as part of the first slide's content is expected behavior for an accessible document title and is not considered a defect. Branding logos (NC DIT) appear multiple times, but this correctly reflects their presence on every slide in the source material.
